title: Dryopteris nigritiana
taxonomy: Dryopteris nigritiana is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or autumn ferns. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Dryopteris nigritiana is further grouped into the genus Dryopteris, which contains around 200 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is characterized by its elongated fronds and a distinctive arrangement of leaflets. The classification of Dryopteris nigritiana is still being studied and refined by botanists.

intro: Dryopteris nigritiana is a species of fern native to the highlands of East Africa, characterized by its dark green, leathery fronds. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris nigritiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
